subject: Side-by-side cross-section comparison of two keycap materials — ABS (thinner walls, shinier top surface) on the left; PBT (thicker walls, matte top surface) on the right. Both caps share a baseline so the wall-thickness contrast reads at-a-glance; cruciform stems hang from each inner ceiling to mark the shapes as keycap cross-sections rather than generic silhouettes.

palette.background: transparent (sits cleanly on the dark-mode charcoal #1d1f24 and on light paper)

motif: two keycap cross-sections, ABS vs PBT, side-by-side
notes: Single-splash warm-ochre line drawing — wall-thickness contrast (10 px ABS vs 16 px PBT inset from outer wall) carries the comparison; ABS top reads shiny via a long dotted-arc highlight, PBT top reads matte via a stipple-dash band. No interior fills, family-convention 2 px stroke weight, brass-bronze ornamental dot at upper-right per the hero set.
